claims.claim: A scalar `viscosity_persistence_coefficient` can under-report whether sudden thickening feels cohesive, granular, or syrupy.
claims.rationale: Implemented additive diagnostic `viscosity_type` and `viscosity_decay_hint` fields on the read-only transport review without replacing the live scalar or changing control.

claims.claim: Replacing the scalar with a struct/vector or changing viscosity decay curves would be a live schema/control-facing change.
claims.rationale: Explicitly authority-gated the replacement/decay-curve path: this run added diagnostic companion fields only and did not alter pressure, fill, PI, porosity, bridge ABI, or controller behavior.

claims.claim: Missing `porosity_gradient` can explain hollow or leaky states and should not be treated as zero porosity.
claims.rationale: Verified the existing transport review preserves `porosity_gradient_unavailable`, and structural transparency review names hollow high-viscosity low-substance states; the new viscosity type works with optional porosity rather than coercing absence to zero.
